mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy degrades to chance levels over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing construction activity combined with rising industrial production signals synchronized demand and supply expansion, which compresses credit spreads and lifts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2015-09 HOUST at 1206.0 and INDPRO at 107.09
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict directionally correct 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in real consumer credit growth signals tightening financial conditions that restrict corporate investment and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the tightening credit signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when tested on post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples adjust to higher fundamental growth.
evidence_refs: fact:2015-07 GDPC1 at 16394.2 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive sequential growth, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 238.0, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses above 238.0, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with declining housing starts indicates a structural shift in capital allocation toward services and away from construction, which increases earnings volatility and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term's predictive power becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial output contraction, a simultaneous decline in real consumer credit signals a synchronized demand shock that overrides supply-side resilience, causing corporate revenue misses and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the industrial and credit contraction, or the signal's directional accuracy fails to exceed 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample evaluation.
